text:
The Paris Games are underway. You can stream every moment on Peacock.



We've got a full slate ahead of us, with plenty of edge-of-your-seat action in store.

Simone Biles will look to continue her incredible comeback journey by adding more gold to her tally, this time in balance beam and floor exercises. She'll be joined on balance beam by fellow American Suni Lee, herself on a comeback journey that almost kept her out of the Games. On floor exercises, another American, Jordan Chiles, will look to challenge for a podium spot.

Here are some other events to look for:

Triathlon

Mixed team relay,2 a.m. ET/8 a.m. Paris

Shooting

Men芒聙聶s rapid fire pistol, 3:30 a.m. ET/9:30 a.m. Paris 脗

Mixed team skeet,9 a.m. ET/3 p.m. Paris

Badminton

Women芒聙聶s singles, gold medal match, 4:55脗聽a.m. ET/10:55 a.m. Paris

Men芒聙聶s singles脗聽medal matches,8:30 a.m. ET/2:30 p.m. Paris

Gymnastics

Men芒聙聶s parallel bars,5:45 a.m. ET/11:45 a.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s balance beam,6:38 a.m. ET/12:38 p.m. Paris

Men芒聙聶s high bar,7:33 a.m. ET/1:33 p.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s floor exercise,8:23 a.m. ET/2:23 p.m. Paris

Canoe Slalom脗

Women芒聙聶s kayak cross, 10:55 a.m. ET/4:55 p.m. Paris

Men芒聙聶s kayak cross, 11 a.m. ET/5 p.m. Paris

Track and Field脗

Men芒聙聶s pole vault, 1 p.m. ET/7 p.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s discus,2:30 p.m. ET/8:30 p.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s 5000m,3:15脗聽p.m. ET/9:15 p.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s 800m,3:47 p.m. ET/9:47 p.m. Paris

Cycling脗

Women芒聙聶s team sprint medal rounds,1:54 p.m. ET/7:54 p.m. Paris脗聽脗

Basketball脗

Women芒聙聶s 3x3 tournament medal games,3 p.m. ET/9 p.m. Paris脗

Men芒聙聶s 3x3 tournament medal games,3:30脗聽p.m. ET/9:30脗聽p.m. Paris

Surfing脗

Men芒聙聶s medal matches,3:24脗聽p.m. ET/9:24 p.m. Paris

Women芒聙聶s medal matches,4:05脗聽p.m. ET/10:05 p.m. Paris

Team USA added five脗gold medals,脗two silver medals and three bronze medals to its tally at the Paris Games today, bringing its total medal count to 71.

The five golds were in track and field, golf, road cycling and swimming; the two silvers were in archery and swimming; and the three bronze were in gymnastics, track and field and skeet shooting.

Twenty medals were handed out today, and the U.S. added half of those to its count.

It is top among competing nations and groups andtied for the most gold medalswith China at 19. The Americans have 19 gold, 26 silver and 26 bronze medals.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Olympic swimmer Katie Ledecky just made history as the most decorated U.S. female Olympic athlete, with 14 medals. She sat down with NBC News芒聙聶 Lester Holt to talk about how her latest victories felt and what she hopes to do next.

Ethan Katzberg won Canada芒聙聶s first gold in hammer throw today.

Katzberg芒聙聶s medal is the first of any color in the event since 1912, when Duncan Gillis won silver. This is the third straight Games where Canada has won at least one track and field gold medal after having gone four consecutive Games without one.

Hungary芒聙聶s Bence Hal脙隆sz won silver, and Ukraine芒聙聶s Mykhaylo Kokhan won bronze.

Hal脙隆sz continues a tradition: Hungary has had six track and field medals in the last 50 years, all of which have been in throwing events.

Meanwhile, this is Ukraine芒聙聶s second medal ever in this event, men芒聙聶s or women芒聙聶s. The first medal went to Oleksandr Krykun, who won silver in 1996.

Ukraine won three track and field medals today after having won only one track and field medal in each of the last two Olympics.

American Rudy Winkler placed sixth in the final, and fellow American Daniel Haugh was eliminated in qualifying.

A芒聙聶ja Wilson led a balanced offensive effort while some ferocious defense helped propel the U.S. to a convincing win over Germany to conclude group play.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Eight men 芒聙聰 the entire starting field 芒聙聰 in the 100 meters today finished in under 10 seconds, a historic first for a wind-legal race, according toWorld Athletics, the governing body for athletics, including track and field.

The race was so tight that the gold medalist, Team USA's Noah Lyles, and the silver medalist, Jamaica's Kishane Thompson, both posted times of 9.79, with Lyles winning the battle of decimal places by 0.005 seconds.

Today's last-place finisher, Jamaica's Oblique Seville, posted a 9.91, which would have been good enough for fourth place at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics,according toOlympicrecords.

Six finalists in Tokyo finished in under 10 seconds, with one runner disqualified and one posting a "did not finish."

A composite image of the eight men competing in the 100-meter final on Sunday at the Paris Olympics.脗Hector Vivas / Getty Images

The photo finish in the men's 100-meter final at the Paris Olympic Games.脗World Athletics

Team USA芒聙聶s Noah Lyles took the gold in脗聽the men芒聙聶s 100-meter final at the Paris Olympics 芒聙聰脗聽by five thousandths of a second.

Lyles, who won today with a time of 9.784 seconds, came out just barely ahead of Jamaica芒聙聶s Kishane Thompson (who ran in 9.789) and USA芒聙聶s Fred Kerley (whose time was 9.81).

The race was so close that even the commentators nearly called the wrong winner before the脗photo finish, which showed Lyles being the first to lean over the finish line even as others managed to step over it before him.

Read the full story here.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Bold American sprinting star Noah Lyles arrived at the Paris Olympics announcing his intentions of leaving with four gold medals.

One down, three to go.

The 27-year-old from Virginia脗won his first gold medal脗today by bolting to the 100-meter title in a personal best 9.79 seconds in front of a deafening, sold-out Stade de France crowd.

A self-described showman who thrives on a large audience, Lyles ran from behind for much of the first 90 meters while chasing down Jamaica芒聙聶s Kishane Thompson to a photo finish. The sprinters waited nervously at the finish line, watching the scoreboard for results.

Lyles froze when it showed he had prevailed by one of the narrowest margins 芒聙聰 five-thousandths of a second.

Read the full story here.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Americans Gerek Meinhardt, Alexander Massialas, Nick Itkin and Miles Chamley-Watson took on France in the bronze match of the men芒聙聶s team foil event. France won 45-32 over the two-time defending bronze medalists.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Snoop Dogg and Martha Stewart took in equestrian competition today, with the rapper showing fascination with the traditions of the sport as it returned to its Olympics birthplace in Paris.

"Only in TV and movies have I seen things of this nature," Snoop, in formal dressage attire, said as he strolled equestrian grounds with Stewart.

The pair took in the grace and fancy footwork of dressage as English rider Becky Moody wowed spectators with her performance atop horse Jagerbomb.

Teammate Charlotte Fry used Queen's "Another One Bites the Dust" for her performance as Snoop and Stewart watched from a studio. The U.K. equestrian team earned bronze in dressage.

Stewart said she has reserved a stall for Snoop at her stable when he's ready to get a horse. Snoop said the time in Paris is helping him get over a fear of horses.

"This is one of my new favorite sports," he said.

U.S. duo Kelly Cheng and Sara Hughes punched their ticket to the Olympic beach volleyball quarterfinals, gutting out a gritty win against Italy芒聙聶s Marta Menegatti and Valentina Gottardi in the Round of 16.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Jessica Fox芒聙聶s quest for a third gold medal at the 2024 Paris Olympics was denied by her own sister in the heats of kayak cross.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Algerian boxer Imane Kheliftook to social mediato congratulate gymnast Kaylia Nemour for hergold medal winin today's uneven bars final.

It's the first Olympic gymnastics medal for any African nation. Both Nemour and Khelif have dealt with obstacles outside the field of play on their journeys to the Olympic podium.

Nemour previously represented France but obtained Algerian citizenship through her paternal ancestry after her home federation tried to bar her from competing. Khelif's gender identitycame under disputeat these Games, but she the IOC cleared her to compete.

芒聙聹I want to tell the entire world that I am a female, and I will remain a female,芒聙聺 Khelif said yesterday afterwinning a matchthat would guarantee her a medal in Paris.

PARIS 芒聙聰脗Suni Lee held down the fort脗聽as the only American in today芒聙聶s uneven bars final, earning a bronze and successfully defending her podium finish in Tokyo.

Algeria芒聙聶s Kaylia Nemour fulfilled her long-awaited destiny by winning gold. Qiu Qiyuan of China took silver.

Lee had the advantage of going last, which gave her the opportunity to perform the most strategic routine possible. She earned a 14.800 with a slightly more conservative routine and became the first U.S. woman to win two Olympic medals in the event.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Read the full story here.

Jimmer Fredette of the U.S. during a men's 3x3 basketball pool round game against France on Friday.脗Lintao Zhang / Getty Images

PARIS 芒聙聰 Former脗college hoops star脗聽and NBA veteran Jimmer Fredette tore ligaments playing for Team USA芒聙聶s 3x3 Olympics team and will be sidelined for about six months, he revealed late today on social media.

The Americans didn芒聙聶t get out of group play and suffered a tournament-ending脗21-6 loss to the Netherlands, landing脗them脗聽in seventh place脗among eight squads.

Fredette said he tore two ligaments in his adductor in his second game in Paris, a loss to Poland. He was on the bench in uniform but didn芒聙聶t play the rest of the way.

芒聙聹This is devastating for me as I have put two years into qualifying for the Olympics with this group who are my brothers,芒聙聺 Fredette said in a statement on Instagram.

Read the full story here.

The last heat in the men芒聙聶s 1,500 meters saw defending Olympic gold medalist Jakob Ingebrigtsen and reigning world champion Josh Kerr face off.

Ingebrigtsen won the heat with a time of 3:32.38, 0.08 faster than Kerr.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Americans Yared Nuguse and Hobbs Kessler finished 1-2 in their 1,500-meter semifinal.

They finished with the top two times of 3:31.72 and 3:31.97, respectively. They will face favorites Ingebrigtsen and Kerr tomorrow.

We apologize, this video has expired.

World record holder Yaroslava Mahuchikh of Ukraine won her first Olympic gold medal in the women芒聙聶s high jump, edging Australia芒聙聶s Nicola Olyslagers, who claimed silver for the second consecutive Olympics.

We apologize, this video has expired.

American Juliette Whittaker posted a time of 1:57.76 in the women芒聙聶s 800-meter semifinal to advance to the final in Paris.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Quarterfinal matchups in women's water polo are set, with the U.S. meeting Hungary on Tuesday.

The game is scheduled to start at 2:35 p.m. ET. The other quarterfinal matchups, all scheduled for Tuesday, are Canada vs. Spain (8 a.m. ET), the Netherlands vs. Italy (9:35 a.m. ET) and Australia vs. Greece (1 p.m. ET).

Semifinals are scheduled for Thursday. Medal matches, including the final, are expected to take place Saturday.

The U.S. and Spain have been the gold medal favorites, and a possible matchup in the final would reprise the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, where the Americans took gold.

Spain beat the women's team in early pool play in Paris 13-11, but the U.S. team, hoping for an unprecedented fourth consecutive gold medal in Olympics water polo, men's or women's, has a greater goal differential 芒聙聰 26 compared to Spain's 15.

Scottie Scheffler joined Mike Tirico to talk about winning gold in the Olympic men芒聙聶s golf event and what it芒聙聶s like to compete in the Games.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Just one day after winning an historic gold medal for the Philippines on the floor exercise, Carlos Yulo picked up a second gold medal on the vault.

We apologize, this video has expired.

NANTERRE, France 芒聙聰 Bobby Finke and the U.S. women set world records in the 1,500-meter freestyle and脗聽the 4x100-meter medley today, adding final touches of glory for Americans, who suffered a relative collapse in the gold market but still took home the most medals from this quadrennial swim meet.

Gold medalist Bobby Finke of the U.S. poses after the men's 1500m freestyle Sunday.脗Jonathan Nackstrand / AFP - Getty Images

The U.S. made脗28 visits to the Paris La D脙漏fense Arena podium, but Americans were on the top step only eight times.

While both numbers were more than any other country's, it was a slight step back by supremely high U.S. standards.

Read the full story here.

U.S. cyclist Kristen Faulkner surprised everyone when she won the women芒聙聶s road race with a time of 3:49:23. Marianne Vos of the Netherlands edged out Lotte Kopecky of Belgium for silver. Chlo脙漏 Dygert芒聙聶s crash affected the race.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Team USA picked up 18 medals yesterday 芒聙聰 five gold, six silver, seven bronze 芒聙聰the most for the U.S. in a single day since Oct. 1, 1988, at the Seoul Games, when the U.S. also won 18.

Entering these Games, only five athletes in Olympic history had ever won four gold medals in the same individual event. Yesterday, two American athletes joined that list in a matter of hours.

Vincent Hancock became the sixth athlete to complete the feat, winning his fourth gold medal in shooting芒聙聶s men芒聙聶s skeet.脗聽Later, swimmer Katie Ledecky won her fourth gold medal in the women's 800-meter freestyle.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Full prime-time coverage begins tonight on NBC and Peacock at 7 p.m. ET.

Noah Lyles won the men's 100 meters by five-thousandths of a second over Jamaica's Kishane Thompson 芒聙聰脗Lyles' torso crossing the line first.

Here's the photo finish.

The photo finish in the men's 100-meter final.脗World Athletics

According to official race data, Noah Lyles had the worst reaction time of the men芒聙聶s 100-meter final, as he was in last place for the first 40 meters and still in seventh at the halfway point.

In fact, he never led the race until the final few steps, pipping by the smallest of margins Kishane Thompson, who was winning for much of the sprint, according to the data.

芒聙聹Oh shhh ...!芒聙聺 Lyles exclaimed, just stopping himself short, when reporters told him about his improbable midrace comeback.

芒聙聹Slowest reaction time? Dang, I芒聙聶m amazing. That芒聙聶s crazy! That芒聙聶s crazy! That just goes to prove that reaction time does not win races.芒聙聺

Team USA芒聙聶s Noah Lyles said he thought Jamaica芒聙聶s Kishane Thompson脗聽had won the 100-meter final when the pair crossed the line five-thousandths of a second apart.

芒聙聹I did think Thompson had it at the end,芒聙聺 Lyle told a scrum of reporters. 芒聙聹I went up to him afterward, while we were waiting. I even said, 芒聙聵Bro I think you got that one, big dog.芒聙聶 And and then my name popped up and I芒聙聶m like, 芒聙聵Oh, my gosh, I芒聙聶m amazing!芒聙聶芒聙聺 he added with a laugh.

When his name did show up on the giant screen in the stadium, Lyles said, it came as a rare surprise for a man never short of self-confidence.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m going to be honest, I wasn芒聙聶t ready to see it,芒聙聺 he said. 芒聙聹And that芒聙聶s the first time I芒聙聶ve ever said that in my head that I wasn芒聙聶t ready to see it.芒聙聺

Thompson 芒聙聹was a few quite a few lanes over 芒聙聰 he芒聙聶s in lane four, I was in lane seven 芒聙聰 so it was hard for me to picture where we were. But I guess that was a good thing.芒聙聺

Crazy but true: Kenny Bednarek芒聙聶s time of 9.88 in the 100-meter sprint would have earned him a medal at the Rio or Tokyo Games.

In Paris, he ended up in seventh.

After losing the 100-meter gold to Noah Lyles by five-thousandths of a second, Jamaica's Kishane Thompson said Lyles went up to him after the race and said he thought that Thompson had won it.

Thompson, on the other hand, didn't have a clue until the results were officially announced.

"I couldn't see him. ... He was to my far right, so I wasn芒聙聶t sure," Thompson told reporters. "My closest race ever."

Asked whether he thought runners should share the gold medal, Thompson quickly dismissed the idea, asserting that the sport was too competitive for such a thing.

Thompson, 23, had run faster times than Lyles this season, including in the Olympic heats leading up to the final. He said he would've loved to win the gold but still felt "super grateful" for the silver at his first Olympics.

After fulfilling part one of his Olympic destiny, Noah Lyles said that for him, there was never any doubt.

"I was always the fastest man in the world," he said after his 100-meter win. "Now I just got the medals to prove it."

Asked whether he has ever run like that before, he said he has "had many races like that, but never at the Olympics."

"I'll tell you that, that was an amazing moment, but it was tight, and it was great," he said.

Noah Lyles claimed the 100 meters in one of the tightest finishes in history. Here's an overhead shot of the finish.

Lyles could be the first sprinter to win the Olympic double since Usain Bolt in 2016 and the first American since Carl Lewis in 1984.

The most difficult test, the 100 meters, is complete, but Thursday he'll also have to win the 200-meter final, which is his signature event. Lyles achieved the elusive feat at last year's world championships.

He could win a third Olympic gold medal when he runs the 4x100-meter relay Thursday.

Amid an insanely talented crop of runners, all three Americans running the 1500-meter race will vie for gold in the final.

American record holder Yared Nuguse was the fastest in the two semifinal heats, clocking in at 3:31.72, with fellow American Hobbs Kessler right behind him.

Finishing first in the other heat was defending Olympic champion Jakob Ingebrigtsen from Norway, followed by defending world champion Josh Kerr from Great Britain, and USA's Cole Hocker in third.

Nuguse, Kessler and Hocker each won a medal at World Indoors in March and will look for their first Olympic medals in what promises to be an exhilarating final on Tuesday.

A moment of outrageous drama here at the Stade de France. First a dazzling light show that illumined the arena in a constellation of purple. Then the athletes came out, with eventual winner Noah Lyles running and jumping down the track to ostentatiously announce his presence.

Then from sheer noise to pin-drop quiet as the runners lined up on the start.

After the starting gun, from the stands it was hard to pick a clear favorite during the sub-10 seconds they were haring down the track. Spectators and competitors alike seemed none the wiser as to who had won, with Lyles only celebrating when the photo finish was declared.

By just five thousandths of a second, Noah Lyles ended a 20-year American gold medal drought in the men's 100-meter sprint.

After a tense review period, Lyles earned his first Olympic gold medal. He's halfway to his goal of winning the Olympic double, which will come down to the 200-meter Thursday.

Upon clinching the gold, he looked straight into the camera and said, "America, I told you."

Team USA芒聙聶s脗聽Noah Lyles wins gold in the men芒聙聶s 100-meter final and Fred Kerley gets bronze!

After fleeing her hometownon the front lines of Russia芒聙聶s invasion, Ukrainian high jumper Yaroslava Mahuchikh has just won gold at the Stade de France.

An almighty roar erupted from the stands when it became clear that Mahuchikh had bested her main rival, Australia芒聙聶s Nicola Olyslagers. So did it too when she rang the Olympic champions bell inside the stadium, which will be hung inside the rebuilt Notre-Dame Cathedral after the Games.

Aside from her remarkable origin story, Mahuchikh has captured the attention this tournament for her unique routine in between jumps: She swaddles herself in a sleeping bag. Olyslagers, meanwhile, furiously writes in her journal.

After an epic gold-medal battle, the pair embraced as soon as the result was confirmed.

After the U.S. women took gold in the team foil event, the men narrowly missed the podium in theirs, losing to France 45-32.

Following strong bouts in the first two relays from USA's Alexander Massialas and individual bronze medalist Nick Itkin, the French fencers dominated the remaining seven, picking up momentum from the energized home crowd and securing the bronze medal.

In the fencing finale of this Olympic Games, Italy and Japan are currently fighting in a tight match for gold.

Scottie Scheffler, Tommy Fleetwood and Hideki Matsuyama accept their medals after the conclusion of the Olympic men芒聙聶s golf event.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Team USA's Nia Akins finished third in her semifinal for the women's 800-meter, but her time of 1:58.20 means she wasn't quick enough to qualify for the final. However, that does mean that her compatriot Juliette Whittaker does go through, with her time of 1:57.76 being enough to make tomorrow night's denouement.

Juliette Whittaker of Team USA gave an extraordinary push on the back straight to finish third in her women's 800-meter semifinal. Only the top two go through automatically, so Whittaker will have to wait until the last round to see if she makes the cut.

While Team USA dominates the 5x5 men's basketball pool, the 3x3 team has struggled, finishing 2 for 5 in the pool rounds and ultimately failing to advance.

After losing its first four games, the 3x3 men's team came back to win their next two, giving themselves a chance to win a third and advance to the play-in rounds. But the Netherlands dashed those hopes quickly with a commanding 21-6 win today.

The U.S. team had been playing shorthanded since losing key player Jimmer Fredette to a leg injury in their second game.

Meanwhile, the women's 3x3 team will look to defend their gold medal from Tokyo and face Spain in the semifinals tomorrow.

With all the heats done, we now know that Kenny Bednarek has qualified for the men's 100-meter final despite not finishing in the top two of his heat. Bednarek and Olympic champion Lamont Marcell Jacobs, of Italy, had the two fastest times of those who did not automatically go through.

That means that three of the eight finalists will be Americans: Bednarek, Noah Lyles and Fred Kerley, who qualified automatically in the other heats.

Jon Rahm was four shots clear of the field on the back nine Sunday at the Olympic men芒聙聶s event, but finished without a medal. He tried to put his emotions into words.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Team USA's Fred Kerley finished second in his semifinal of the men's 100-meter sprint, meaning he joins fellow countryman Noah Lyle in the final.

In the second semifinal for the men's 100-meter sprint, Team USA's Kenny Bednarek has come in fourth, meaning he will have to hope he is among the fastest also-rans if he has any hope of qualifying for the final.

Only the top two go through from each heat, plus the next fastest two runners.

Even after winning gold in Tokyo, Chinese Taipei duo Yang Lee and Chi-lin Wang were not the favorites to win men's badminton doubles in Paris. China's Wei Keng Liang and Chang Wang loomed large as the No. 1-ranked pair in the world after winning the Asian Championships earlier this year.

But Lee and Wang prevailed, winning an extremely close, emotionally charged three-game match against their Chinese opponents, and clinching their second straight gold medal in the event. Overcome with emotion, Lee and Wang collapsed to the ground as their supporters erupted into a standing ovation.

We apologize, this video has expired.

American sprinter Noah Lyles bagged a comfortable second place in the men's 100-meter semifinal, earning him a place in the final later this evening. It was a photo finish for first place with Jamaica's Oblique Seville, who won the race, but neither man's place in the final looked in doubt.

It芒聙聶s a beautiful evening at the Stade de France, where crowds are arriving for an evening session of athletics capped by the men芒聙聶s 100-meter final.

Golden sunshine, high cirrus clouds and a pleasant 77 degrees are a recipe for good vibes at the Olympic Stadium north of Paris.

On the road to the arena, food trucks selling 10-euro pints of 芒聙聹bi脙篓re blonde芒聙聺 are circumventingthe Games芒聙聶 abstemious no-booze policy, and seemingly every nation芒聙聶s colors can be seen among the masses traipsing the 15 minutes from the Metro to the bleachers.

American women set a world record in the 4x100-meter medley, pulling away to win the final swimming race of the Paris Olympics.

The time of 3:49.62 topped silver Australia and bronze China.

The race broke the tie for gold medals between the U.S. and Australia. Both nations entered the final race with seven gold medals in swimming in Paris before the Americans emerged victorious.

PARIS 芒聙聰 Belgium芒聙聶s Olympic committee announced Sunday that it would withdraw its team from the mixed relay triathlon at the Paris Olympics after one of its competitors who swam脗in the Seine River脗聽fell ill.

Claire Michel, who competed脗in the women芒聙聶s triathlon Wednesday,脗聽芒聙聹is unfortunately ill and will have to withdraw from the competition,芒聙聺 the Belgian Olympic and Interfederal Committee said in a statement.

The mixed relay triathlon is scheduled for Monday, with the swim portion of the competition slated for the Seine as well. The statement did not elaborate on Michel芒聙聶s illness but it comes after concerns over the脗river芒聙聶s water quality.

Nigeria made history earlier today as the first African team, men's or women's, to reach the Olympic basketball quarterfinals.

We apologize, this video has expired.

The 12th-ranked Nigerians won 79-70 over fifth-ranked Canada, fueled by a game-high 21 points from Ezinne Kalu. Canada was left winless in three games and eliminated.

Pan Zhanle anchored China to a thrilling come-from-behind win in the men芒聙聶s swimming 4x100 medley relay, rallying past the United States and France.

The U.S. and hosts were neck and neck headed into the freestyle before Pan芒聙聶s time of 45.92 put his team past American Hunter Armstrong (47.19) and Frenchman脗聽Florent Manaudou (47.59).

The 4x100 medley is considered one of America芒聙聶s strongest脗聽events, with the U.S. having captured gold in 15 of 16 Olympic finals before tonight.

American Bobby Finkedefended his 1500-meter freestyle titlein word-record fashion, winning a second consecutive gold in the arduous race.脗

The Tampa native pulled away from Italy芒聙聶s Gregorio Paltrinieri in the final 50 and touched the wall at 14:30.67, besting the previous mark held by China芒聙聶s Sun Wang, who had a time of 14:31.02 in 2012.

Ireland芒聙聶s Daniel Wiffen, considered the favorite here, took bronze.

Despite an impressive first two games in its first-ever Olympics, the German women's basketball team couldn't come close to breaking the U.S. women's formidable winning streak, as the U.S. cruised to an 87-68 victory.

A'ja Wilson and Breanna Stewart each scored in the double digits, but the star of the game was Jackie Young, who exploded for a game-high 19 points, including five 3-pointers. The U.S. showed off its depth, with its bench contributing 52 points compared to Germany's 13.

The U.S. will next play in the quarterfinals on Wednesday, as it pursues its eighth straight Olympic gold medal.

Swedish speedster Sarah Sj脙露str脙露m splashed and dashed to gold in the 50-meter freestyle.

Sj脙露str脙露m was the overwhelming favorite here, having set an Olympic record in the semifinals last night. Australia's Meg Harris took silver and China's Zhang Yufei bronze.

Nashville native Gretchen Walsh finished just off the podium in fourth.

The world's No. 1 golfer, Scottie Scheffler, has added an Olympic gold medal to his impressive resume, which includes his second Masters title from earlier this year.

Entering the final round four shots behind leaders Jon Rahm and defending Olympic champion Xander Schauffele, Scheffler staged a dramatic comeback. Rahm and Schauffele faltered as Scheffler fired four straight birdies and tied a course record of 9 under 62 to win the men's competition.

Great Britain's Tommy Fleetwood fell out of the lead with a bogey and landed silver, while Japan's Hideki Matsuyama took bronze.

We apologize, this video has expired.

It was the "upset of a lifetime" in women's road cycling today as AmericanKristen Faulknersurged ahead of the pack to secure gold at the end of the race.

Faulkner was not a favorite to win this race, but the former rower crossed the finish line almost a full minute ahead of Netherlands' Marianne Vos and Germany's Lotte Kopecky.

This is the first Olympics for Faulkner, who did not qualify on her own for the race and was added to Team USA at the last minute. She only began cycling as a hobby, saying it provided an outlet for her venture capital job in New York City.

Faulkner is the first American woman to win gold in the women芒聙聶s road race since 1984.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Read the full article here

After Germany appeared to be stiff competition at the start of the game, the U.S. women's basketball team remembered who they were in the second quarter.

The U.S. hit their stride with a sharp 11-0 run fueled by improved shooting and more aggression in the paint. But the team still has room for cohesion, and they might be lucky that Germany's 3-pointers went cold.

After a sluggish start, A'ja Wilson found her form again, combining for 19 points with Breanna Stewart.

Germany set the tone early, with three consecutive shots from downtown to continue their strong 3-point shooting in Paris so far. The U.S. continued to struggle with theirs, missing all three of their attempts in the first quarter.

Breanna Stewart leads the U.S. in scoring with 6 points.

Did Suni Lee think she would be here at Paris 2024 given her recent battles with kidney disease?

芒聙聹Absolutely not,芒聙聺 she said. 芒聙聹In the back of my head, I really thought that I was done.芒聙聺 Gymnastics 芒聙聹wasn芒聙聶t even something that I wanted to do anymore. I was ready to move on with my life. I wanted to move across the country, and everyone芒聙聶s like, 芒聙聵What are you talking about? Like you can芒聙聶t make decisions when you芒聙聶re sad,芒聙聶芒聙聺 she added with a laugh.

The result, however, is a Games where she and her fellow Team USA gymnasts are clearly in a better place than at Tokyo 2020. 芒聙聹You could look back and see a lot of the girls not really having a lot of fun and not smiling,芒聙聺 she said. 芒聙聹And this time we芒聙聶re celebrating after every turn, going up and hugging everybody.芒聙聺

Beaming, she said that 芒聙聹gymnastics is a lot more fun when you芒聙聶re having fun. So I芒聙聶m just having a blast here.芒聙聺

Lee also revealed the inspirational effect she芒聙聶s had on others.

芒聙聹The past couple of days I芒聙聶ve been flooded with messages from people saying they芒聙聶ve had chronic illnesses,芒聙聺 she said. 芒聙聹So it芒聙聶s a really good feeling, knowing that I can inspire others, especially knowing I芒聙聶ve spent a lot of months at my lowest. Having people who were there to uplift me and support me along the way was such a good feeling. And I think that芒聙聶s such an important message: to just lean on your people when you need them.芒聙聺

With its final group play game against Germany underway, the U.S. women's basketball team aims to extend its remarkable streak of 57 straight Olympic wins, dating back to the 1992 Barcelona Games. But Germany is expected to be tough competition, as it also enters the matchup 2-0 with Dallas Wings star Satou Sabally leading the charge.

The winner of this game will emerge as the champion of Group C, though both the U.S. and Germany have already secured spots in the quarterfinals.

Boxer Cindy Ngamba not only earned her personal glory at the Paris Games, but the Cameroon-born athlete has guaranteed the Refugee Olympic Team its first medal.

She defeated France's Davina Michel today, moving forward to a gold medal match in Women's 75-kg boxing. Ngamba is guaranteed a bronze medal regardless of how her next bout in the ring ends.

Ngamba left Cameroon for the U.K. with her mother and brother when she was just 11, where she said she struggled with the English-language. She is also openly gay, something she could not be in her country of origin as homosexuality is illegal in Cameroon.

At the end of her fight against Michel, which Ngamba won by unanimous decision, she grabbed a board and pointed to the Refugee Olympic Team name for the camera and the crowds.

After winning bronze in women芒聙聶s uneven bars, Team USA芒聙聶s Suni Lee told reporters thatafter a challenging time battling kidney disease, her main goal today was redemption.

芒聙聹I told myself that I was coming back to redeem myself on bars and that芒聙聶s what I did this time; I really wanted to just put a good, clean routine together,芒聙聺 said Lee. 芒聙聹I saw that I could if I just did the routine that I did the past couple of days, then I could have medaled. I just wanted to go up there and prove to myself that I can do it.芒聙聺

Some may think Lee had an advantage in going last because she knew what she needed to do. But she said 芒聙聹it芒聙聶s a lot harder because I had to watch everybody in front of me, so it definitely put the pressure on.芒聙聺

Lee芒聙聶s teammate Simone Bileswarned journalists earlier in the dayto 芒聙聹stop asking athletes what芒聙聶s next after they win a medal at the Olympics.芒聙聺 When NBC News asked Lee whether she could return at Los Angeles 2028, she locked her stare, gave a half smile, waited a beat and said, 芒聙聹No comment.芒聙聺

The race for a vault medal came down to tenths of a point, the equivalent of a foot shuffle upon landing. Artur Davtyan earned Armenia its second ever Olympic gymnastics medal. The only other one also belongs to him.

Twenty-four hours after winning the first Olympic gymnastics medal for the Philippines, Carlos Yulo has won a second gold, this time in vault.

芒聙聹I am happy that I won a gold medal and also a house," he said after yesterday's floor exercise win, referring to the nation's policy of gifting Olympic gold medal winners a home.

He has yet to share whether this means he gets two houses.

Alcaraz fell 7-6, 7-6 to Novak Djokovic but still is proud of what he was able to accomplish.

芒聙聹It is painful to lose the way that I lost this match," he said on NBC after the defeat. "I had my opportunities. ... Novak is playing great. He deserved this. In the difficult moments, he increased his level. He played unbelievable.芒聙聺

芒聙聹I am a little bit disappointed, but I芒聙聶m going to leave the court with my head high. I gave everything that I had. Fighting for Spain was everything for me. I芒聙聶m proud of the way I played today.芒聙聺

The 37-year-old Serb, who had hinted that this would be his last Olympics, has finally added an elusive gold medal to his arsenal of Grand Slams and trophies.

It was his fifth attempt to take gold.

After winning two sets against Carlos Alcaraz, each with a neck-and-neck tiebreaker, Djokovic did it, defeating his Spanish opponent 7-6, 7-6.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Kaylia Nemour's uneven bars gold is the first ever Olympic gymnastics medal for an African country.

Nemour was born and raised France, but is representing her father's Algeria in Paris due to a dispute with her home country's federation. Sheswitched nationalities last year脗聽and qualified for the Paris Games despite Algeria not fielding a full gymnastics team.

The French team is sure to be lamenting burned bridges with Nemour this week. They had a disastrous showing in front of their home crowd in qualifying and failed to advance to any finals.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Novak Djokovic is three games away from a gold medal. But he's fighting tooth and nail to get there.

With the second set underway, the Serb and the Spaniard are tied 4-4.

As the Paris heat beats down on them, it's clear Carlos Alcaraz is losing focus.

Meanwhile, Djokovic has just about every honor in tennis except Olympic gold. He is halfway there to getting it, but facing an uphill climb, as winning each point becomes a herculean task for either player.

Team USA's Austen Smith has won bronze in the women's skeet shooting event.

This is 23-year-old Smith's second Olympic Games, finishing 10th in this event in Tokyo, and her medal follows yesterday's men's skeet event in which Team USA's Vincent Hancock and Conner Prince took gold and silver.

Ahead of Team USA's big hope in the uneven bars, the stadium built to a crescendo roar before dying back down to almost complete silence as Suni Lee began her routine. Shouts of "come on, Suni!" could be heard from the crowd, which erupted when she landed on the mat.

When her score came through on the big screen, enough to gain a bronze medal, Lee visibly gasped, the audience saluting her with rapturous applause.

Lee's suitcase is getting heavy in Paris. She claimed the bronze in Sunday's bars final, her third medal of these Olympics and sixth total.

She had the advantage of going last, which gave her the opportunity to perform the most strategic routine possible. Lee earned a 14.800 with a slightly more conservative routine, clinching her second consecutive bronze in the event.

Upon landing her dismount on her signature event, Kaylia Nemour couldn't keep the emotion from her face.

Her road to Paris was a long one. She weathered a dispute with her home country of France that led to a switch in national delegation to her father's Algeria. She is representing Algeria here and is now in a position to secure the country their first medal in gymnastics.

It's likely to be gold. She earned a 15.700, the highest score on the event of the entire Olympics.

The uneven bars podium has a new leader: Qiu Qiyuan of China.

Qiyuan has struggled with consistency in Paris, falling in Thursday's all-around final, but not today. She earned a 15.500, putting her in first place by a wide margin ahead of the reigning Olympic champion, Nina Derwael.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Becky Downie is a three-time Olympian, but an Olympic medal continues to elude her.

She is a world medalist on bars, but fell on her routine in Sunday's final, an error that will likely keep her from the podium.

Competing in Paris at all is a triumph for Downie, who suddenly lost her brother ahead of the Tokyo trials and failed to make the team that went on to win a historic team bronze. Simone Biles could be seen covering her face in the crowd after Downie's mistake, a testament to Downie's international popularity in the sport.

The Bercy Arena let out a collective gasp as Team GB's Rebecca Downie lost her grip and fell onto the mat during her uneven bars final routine. She scored 13.633, putting her in third place so far.

Belgium's Nina Derwael, who won the uneven bars gold medal in Tokyo, narrowly earned a Paris berth after dislocating her shoulder.

Her 14.766 will overtake Alice D'Amato, but she has yet to return to peak form.

She had to qualify on her weaker event, the balance beam, to make it to Paris, but easily earned her spot in this bar final.

After narrowly missing the all-around podium Thursday, Italy's Alice D'Amato had a strong showing in Sunday's uneven bars final, earning a 14.733.

She's in first for now, but many contenders have yet to go, including Suni Lee and Algeria's Kaylia Nemour, who is favored for gold.

The gold-medal match between Carlos Alcaraz and Novak Djokovic is neck and neck.脗聽It芒聙聶s a rematch of last month芒聙聶s Wimbledon final, from which Alcaraz walked away victorious.

After 13 unsuccessful break points, the first set moved into a tiebreaker, during which the players alternated scoring. Djokovic was on the attack, going for winners more than once during points sometimes. Alcaraz had a number of unforced errors that flopped on execution. But at moments, you could see the 21-year-old Spaniard getting under Djokovic芒聙聶s skin, as he got frustrated with the closeness of the set.

Ultimately, Djokovic won the tiebreaker and took the first set after more than 90 minutes of battling. Whoever takes the best of three will take home the脗聽gold.

The athletes are out warming up for the uneven bars, and Team USA's big hope, Suni Lee, just got a huge reaction from the Stars and Stripes-waving section of the crowd when her name was called out.

China secured some redemption in today's men's still rings final after narrowly losing the top spot in the all-around and team finals to Japan.

Team USA has one chance to medal on the uneven bars final today, and that one chance is named Suni Lee.

Lee is in third after qualifiers with a score of 14.866, trailing Kaylia Nemour in first and Qiu Qiyuan in second.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Nemour is the gold-medal favorite today, and while both Qiu and Lee are expected to medal, it's unclear who will grab silver and who will take bronze.

Qiu was the likely contender for silver heading into Paris, but she's struggled with consistency throughout these Games, notably falling in the all-around final Thursday in her signature event.

Lee has been stunning in all of her bars performances so far, one of which helped her team win the gold medal on Tuesday and the other earning her a bronze in the all-around on Thursday.

Five millimeters is all that separated Team USA's Brady Ellison from achieving his dream of an Olympic gold medal, but the Arizona native will have to settle for silver after losing to South Korea's Kim Woojin by the tiniest of margins.

With the scores level after five sets, the gold came down to a one-arrow shoot-off, with whoever's arrow landed closer to the center of the target taking the gold. Kim went first, landing his arrow on the outer edge of the 10, but in response, Ellison's arrow was heartbreakingly just five millimeters farther away from the center.

The result means Ellison takes silver, his second medal of these Games and the fifth of his career 芒聙聰 his tally now standing at three silvers and two bronzes.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Carlos Alcaraz has fought back to make it even at 3-3 in the first set of the gold-medal match against Novak Djokovic.

Novak Djokovic has an early advantage in the first set, 2-1, over Carlos Alcaraz as the two men battle for gold.

Team USA's Brady Ellison is into the gold medal match of the men's individual archery competition after beating Germany芒聙聶s Florian Unruh 7-3 in the semifinals.

Ellison claimed his fourth career Olympic medal, with a bronze in the mixed competition alongside Casey Kaufhold earlier in the Games. Now in his fifth Olympics, the Arizona native has two Olympic silvers and two bronzes to his name. At a minimum, he will add another silver to his haul, but he will now have the chance to secure the elusive Olympic gold.

Ellison has been in hot form all day, earlier beating South Korean second seed Kim Je Doek 6-0. He will now face top seed Kim Woojin, also from South Korea, in the gold-medal match shortly at around 8:45 a.m. ET. Kim is searching for his third gold medal of these Games, having already taken the men's team and mixed team titles.

Watch the MVP of these Olympic Games, Snoop Dogg, attempt to weightlift with Team USA.

We apologize, this video has expired.

The packed house at Roland-Garros' Court Philippe-Chatrier erupted moments earlier when Carlos Alcaraz and Novak Djokovic made their way onto the court.

Novak Djokovic and Carlos Alcaraz ahead of the men's singles final tennis match during the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.脗Carl De Souza / AFP - Getty Images

The duo, who faced off just last month at the Wimbledon final, with Alcaraz prevailing, will compete for the gold medal.

Cyclist Kristen Faulkneris set to compete in the women's road race this morning, but her journey to the Olympics is surprising.

She didn芒聙聶t even start cycling until after college. But her hobby of taking rides in New York City芒聙聶s Central Park quickly turned into a competitive passion that landed her on Team USA.

We apologize, this video has expired.

The leading pack are in the early stages of the final round of the golf competition, and Team USA's Scottie Scheffler has started the day brilliantly, birdieing the first three holes.

He is currently tied for third on the leaderboard, one shot behind joint leaders Xander Schauffele and Jon Rahm.

We apologize, this video has expired.

The duo of Cristina Busca and Sara Sorribes Tormo took home the bronze in straight sets today at Roland Garros against Karolina Muchova and Linda Noskova from Czechia.

The Spanish pair won both sets 6-2 in just over an hour to capture the medal.

Up next here at the famed Court Philippe-Chatrier is the highly anticipated men's gold medal match between Carlos Alcaraz and Novak Djokovic.

The match-up between Team USA's Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone and the Netherlands' Femke Bol is one of the most anticipated rivalries on the track of these Games.

Both are safely through to Tuesday's semifinals with McLaughlin-Levrone taking it very easy, strolling over the line in 53.60s. Bol, fresh from making headlines last night after overhauling three runners in the 4x400m mixed relay to take gold in an epic race, took her heat in 53.38s.

We apologize, this video has expired.

McLaughlin-Levrone, the defending Olympic champion, has rewritten the record books in this event, breaking her own world record at the U.S. Olympic trials earlier this year. Bol then went on to run the third-fastest time ever in the event last month, setting up a tantalizing clash at these Games.

Anna Cockrell and NCAA champion Jasmine Jones both won their heats to ensure all three Americans in this event progress.

When Novak Djokovic takes to the court to face off with Carlos Alcaraz at Roland-Garros today, he will be looking to win the one title that has eluded him his entire career 芒聙聰 Olympic gold.

Victory would see 24-time Grand Slam champion Djokovic complete what is known in tennis as the "Golden Slam" 芒聙聰 winning all four Grand Slam titles and Olympic gold across a career. He would be joining the illustrious company of Steffi Graff, Andre Agassi, Rafael Nadal and Serena Williams in the record books.

"This dream is long lived and fought for," Djokovic posted on X after his semifinal victory against Lorenzo Musetti Friday, which saw Djokovic at his glittering best despite concerns over his knee. Certainly, the Olympics have been a brutal beast for the Serbian icon 芒聙聰 a bronze in 2008 was the only Olympic medal to his name before today. At 37-years-old, this may well be Djokovic's last chance to put his Olympic demons to rest.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Spanish phenom Alcaraz, who only turned 21 in May, will be aiming to create history of his own. He is looking to become just the second man ever, behind his compatriot Nadal, to win the French Open, Wimbledon and Olympic gold in the same summer.

This will be a rematch of last month's Wimbledon final with Alcaraz the comfortable straight-sets victor on the grass in London. It was miraculous that Djokovic was even at Wimbledon, let alone the final, with it coming a matter of weeks after Djokovic underwent knee surgery.

The gold medal match of the men's singles is due to begin at around 8 a.m. ET.

Grant Holloway, the three-time reigning World Champion, is safely through to the semifinals of the men芒聙聶s 110m hurdles, but there was a bizarre moment involving one of his Team USA teammates.

Holloway narrowly missed out on gold in Tokyo and is the heavy favorite to earn redemption this time around. He dominated his heat to qualify fastest in 13.01s, the fastest time by more than two-tenths of a second in the heats.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Daniel Roberts scraped through to Wednesday's semifinals, taking a three-way photo finish to scrape through his heat in third.

Freddie Crittenden, who came fourth at last year's World Championships, strangely jogged his heat, saying afterwards he strategically chose to take it easy after tweaking something in the build-up. He came in dead last and will now have until Tuesday to recover when he will have a second chance to make the semifinals in the repechage, essentially a second stage of heats for those who failed to qualify first time around.

Holloway, Roberts and Crittenden have run the three quickest times in the world this year, and there is hope that Team USA could sweep the podium in this event.

Cristina Busca and Sara Sorribes Tormo took the first set 6-2 from Karolina Muchova and Linda Noskova of Czechia in the bronze medal match of women's doubles.

Busca and Tormo got off to a similar start in the second set and took the first two games there as well.

Julien Alfred, 20, stunned Sha芒聙聶Carri Richardson in the women芒聙聶s 100m final, winning St. Lucia芒聙聶s first Olympic medal with a run of 10.72 seconds.脗

We apologize, this video has expired.

The final heat of the women's 200-meter has just wrapped and all three Americans competing in the event have progressed to tomorrow evening's semifinals, with Gabby Thomas with the fastest overall time.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Thomas, who won the bronze medal for this event in Tokyo, will be joined in the semifinals by 2019 world silver medalist Brittany Brown. Thomas' 22.20s was the quickest time in this morning's heats with Brown fourth fastest, winning her heat in 22.38s.

McKenzie Long also won her heat, finishing in 22.55s to qualify with the eighth fastest time.

Lin Yu-ting won her quarterfinal bout against Bulgarian Svetlana Staneva in a unanimous decision that assured a medal for the featherweight representing Chinese Taipei.

Lin andAlgeria芒聙聶s Imane Khelifhave faced withering scrutiny during these Olympics, based on a 2023 disqualifications by theRussian-led International Boxing Associationwhich claimed that unspecified tests called the boxers芒聙聶 gender into question.脗

IOC has staunchly defended the boxers, saying concerns over their gender are baseless. The IBA has since been called into question, with USA Boxingterminated its relationshipwith the body last year.

Read the full story here

Huge news from the Stade de France with Jamaica's Shericka Jackson pulling out of the 200-meter, minutes before the heats this morning.

Jackson, who has won back-to-back world titles in this event, had pulled out of the 100-meter earlier this week. No official reason has been given for her withdrawal from this event yet, though she had been struggling with an injury coming into the Games after pulling up at the Hungarian Grand Prix three-weeks ago.

It is another blow for Jamaica after Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce withdrew from her 100-meter semifinal last night because of an injury.

Team USA's Gabby Thomas is safely through to tomorrow's semifinal, winning her heat in a time of 22.20s. Jackson was set to be her main competition for gold in this event, and her withdrawal really opens up the competition for the rest of the field. Saint Lucia's Julien Alfred, fresh from winning gold in the 100-meter last night, eased into victory in her heat in 22.41s to also progress into the semifinal.

Team USA's Gabby Thomas is involved in this morning's 200-meter heats, which are about to get underway at the Stade de France.

Thomas, who took bronze in Tokyo, is in the second of the six heats.

Jamaica's Shericka Jackson has been the woman to beat in this event in recent years and is Thomas' main rival for gold. She goes in the third heat.

A typical day for U.S. track and field athlete Gabby Thomas is a full 24 hours. During the day, she trains three to six hours in anticipation of the脗2024 Paris Olympics.

But at night, she works at an Austin, Texas, volunteer health care clinic for people without insurance.

How does one of the fastest athletes in her sport find the time to do it all? She attributes that work ethic to her mother. When Thomas and her twin brother were young, their mom waitressed and took classes to become a professor.脗

芒聙聹She showed me in real time growing up what it芒聙聶s like to go after your dreams and to achieve them, and to become successful,芒聙聺 Thomas, 27, told NBC News. 芒聙聹I watched her just achieve all of that by herself and while raising us.芒聙聺

Read the full story here.

With 20 gold medals due to be handed out today, Team USA is hoping to bring in a big medal haul with superstar Noah Lyles headlining one of the most eagerly-anticipated events of the Games 芒聙聰 the men's 100-meters.

Lyles has claimed throughout the build-up to the Olympics he is the fastest man on the planet after winning the world title last year, but with Jamaica's Kishane Thompson and Oblique Seville also in the mix, this looks set to be one of the most hyped and competitive 100-metre races since the days of Usain Bolt.

It's the final day in the pool, and the U.S.-Australia rivalry is on a knife edge. Team USA has already guaranteed they will walk away from the pool with the most total medals 芒聙聰 25 and counting 芒聙聰 but in terms of golds, Australia are narrowly in front with seven golds to the U.S.'s six.

There are some very strong events in the pool for Team USA today though; the U.S. has won every gold medal in the men's 4x100m medley relay since 1960 (except for the boycotted 1980 Games) and will be the favorites for gold again.

Bobby Finke goes in the men's 1500m freestyle as he looks to defend his Olympic title. Team USA and Australia are also expected to clash for gold in the women's 50m freestyle and the women's 4x100m medley relay.

Elsewhere, Suni Lee is involved in the women's uneven bars final, Xander Schauffele is tied for the lead in the men's golf heading into the final round and Team USA are expected to be in the medal mix in fencing's men's team foil event.

The men's team foil medal bouts in fencing take place today.

Like the pommel horse,脗聽fencing also has its origins in military training before slowly evolving into a sport.

The earliest depiction of swordplay is a relief in the temple of Med脛芦nat Habu, built by Egyptian pharaoh Ramses III in about 1190 BCE, near to the Egyptian city of Luxor.

But it wasn芒聙聶t until two-and-a-half thousand years later that it became a sport.

Inspired by the Italian, German and French fencing masters of the early 15th century, it made the transition from a form of military training to sport. German fencing masters then began organizing the first guilds, including the famous Marxbrueder of Frankfurt in 1478.

By the 17th and 18th centuries, the sport of fencing had already begun to resemble what is seen at the Olympics today, with the invention of the flat-tipped foil weapon and wire-mesh masks, as well as rules governing procedure and target area.

The stage is set for an epic finale in the men's golf competition with Team USA's Xander Schauffele tied with Jon Rahm at the top of the leaderboard heading into today's finely-poised final round at Le Golf National.

Schauffele and Rahm are level on 14-under-par with 18 holes left to play, and some of the biggest names in the sport are close behind in the chasing pack.

Great Britain's Tommy Fleetwood is just one shot back, while Team USA's Scottie Scheffler, Ireland's Rory McIlroy and Japan's Hideki Matsuyama are all in the top 10 and can propel themselves into medal contention with strong final rounds.

The final round is already underway with the leading trio of Schauffele, Rahm and Fleetwood the last to tee off for the day at 6:39 a.m. ET.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Saturday was a historic day for Team USA, who added five golds, six silvers and seven bronze medals 芒聙聰 the most medals won by U.S. athletes in a single day since the Seoul Games in 1988.

Among them, Simone Biles won her seventh career Olympic gold in the vault. Vincent Hancock and Katie Ledecky made history by becoming just the sixth and seventh Olympians to win four gold medals in the same individual event 芒聙聰 Hancock doing so in the men芒聙聶s skeet shooting and Ledecky winning her fourth career 800m freestyle gold. Ryan Crouser took his third consecutive gold medal in the men芒聙聶s shot put.

Stream every moment and every medal of the2024 Paris Olympics on Peacock.

Full primetime coverage begins tonight on NBC and Peacock at 7 p.m. ET.

Gymnasts never fail to deliver mesmerizing performances, seamlessly pairing athleticism with grace. The best moves from Simone Biles, Suni Lee and others in the women芒聙聶s individual all-around are even more epic in slow motion.

We apologize, this video has expired.

When Simone Biles does the Yurchenko double pike, the most difficult vault ever done by a female gymnast, the whole world watches in awe. Relive the two phenomenal vaults that earned Biles her 10th Olympic medal.

We apologize, this video has expired.

Day 8 of the Olympics was jam-packed with action. We saw legends in their sport add to their gold-medal counts, thrilling finishes on the track and much more. Let芒聙聶s dive into the day芒聙聶s biggest events.

Read the full story here.

title: 2024 Olympics: Noah Lyles wins 100m in photo finish; Novak Djokovic wins first gold in tennis
description: Follow live updates and the latest news on Day 9 of the 2024 Paris Olympics.
